message:
perf(core,types,vdbe): learn and cache precomputed record headers at runtime for prepared direct inserts

Repeated INSERT statements that hit the prepared-direct-simple-insert
fast path now learn a reusable record-header template from the first
execution and reuse it for subsequent rows, skipping the per-row
header-size computation when all column serial types stay in the
single-byte varint envelope.

Record type system (record.rs):
- Add PrecomputedSerialTypeKind::AnyOneByteVarintOrNull variant that
  covers NULL, all INTEGER encodings, REAL, and short TEXT/BLOB values
  whose serial-type codes fit in a single byte (<128)
- Implement serial_byte_and_payload_len, max_payload_len, header_varint
  _len, and needs_runtime_patch for the new variant so the existing
  precomputed-header fast path can use it
- Add public try_build_runtime_precomputed_record_header() that
  inspects a concrete value slice and returns a PrecomputedRecordHeader
  if every column's serial type fits in one byte — returns None
  otherwise, so the caller silently falls back to the standard path

Connection layer (connection.rs):
- Add cached_record_header: RefCell<Option<PrecomputedRecordHeader>>
  field to PreparedDirectSimpleInsert, initialized to None
- On the hot insert path, attempt
  serialize_record_iter_with_precomputed_header_into() when a cached
  header exists; if it returns false (schema drift or value exceeds
  the one-byte envelope), fall back to serialize_record_iter_into()
  and leave the cache for next time
- After the first successful standard serialization, call
  try_build_runtime_precomputed_record_header() to populate the cache
  for all subsequent rows
- Extract apply_prepared_direct_insert_memdb_row() helper to
  consolidate the MemDatabase table placeholder creation and
  upsert_row call with the required reload-flag bookkeeping
- Add test: test_prepared_direct_simple_insert_learns_runtime_record
  _header_for_small_rows — verifies the cache starts empty and is
  populated after first execution

VDBE engine (engine.rs):
- Add AnyOneByteVarintOrNull arm to estimate_make_record_buffer
  _capacity (uses MAKE_RECORD_VARIABLE_WIDTH_RESERVE_MIN)
- Update hash_program discriminant mapping to include the new variant
  (renumbers IntegerOrNull → 2, RealOrNull → 3)
- Fix two test sites where `retained` binding needed `mut` after
  upstream retained-overlay refactor

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
